classesOfSupply.title: Subsistence
classesOfSupply.description: Subsistence (rations) and gratuitous health and welfare items consumed by personnel at an approximately uniform daily rate.
classesOfSupply.planningNotes: Plan by personnel headcount and operational tempo; typically one of the most predictable consumption classes. Consider local market support and host-nation subsistence supply where appropriate.

classesOfSupply.title: Clothing, Individual Equipment, Tools, Tentage, Organizational Tool Sets and Kits, Hand Tools, Administrative and Housekeeping Supplies and Equipment
classesOfSupply.description: Items of clothing, individual and organizational equipment, tools, tentage, sets, and kits.
classesOfSupply.planningNotes: Forecast based on initial issue, attrition rate, and theater-specific environmental requirements (cold-weather, hot-weather, MOPP).

classesOfSupply.title: Petroleum, Oils, and Lubricants (POL)
classesOfSupply.description: Petroleum products including bulk POL (III B) and packaged POL (III P): fuels, lubricants, hydraulic and insulating oils, preservatives, liquids and gases, bulk chemical products, coolants, deicing and antifreeze compounds.
classesOfSupply.planningNotes: Highest-volume sustainment class for mechanized/aviation operations. Plan ROM/FARP architecture; segregate bulk (III B) from packaged (III P). Distinct distribution networks frequently required.

classesOfSupply.title: Construction and Barrier Materials
classesOfSupply.description: Construction materials, including installed equipment, and all fortification and barrier materials.
classesOfSupply.planningNotes: Heavy by weight/volume; coordinate with engineer planners early. Often constrained by lift and host-nation availability.

classesOfSupply.title: Ammunition
classesOfSupply.description: Ammunition of all types, including chemical, biological, radiological, and special weapons; bombs; explosives; mines; fuses; detonators; pyrotechnics; missiles; rockets; propellants; and other associated items.
classesOfSupply.planningNotes: Tied tightly to scheme of fires and weapons mix. Plan ASPs, FATPs, and convoy security separately. Strict accountability and explosive safety quantity-distance constraints.

classesOfSupply.title: Personal Demand Items
classesOfSupply.planningNotes: Often handled through AAFES or DLA; lower operational priority but morale impact during sustained operations.

classesOfSupply.title: Major End Items
classesOfSupply.description: Major end items, a final combination of end products which is ready for its intended use (combat-ready).
classesOfSupply.planningNotes: Combat losses replaced through Class VII; coordinate via PBO/HRO and theater materiel manager. Typically requires fielding and new equipment training cycles.

classesOfSupply.title: Medical Materiel
classesOfSupply.description: Medical materiel including repair parts peculiar to medical equipment.
classesOfSupply.planningNotes: Distinct medical logistics (MEDLOG) chain through medical brigade or higher. Cold-chain handling for blood and refrigerated pharmaceuticals.

classesOfSupply.title: Repair Parts and Components
classesOfSupply.description: Repair parts and components, including kits, assemblies, and subassemblies, repairable and nonrepairable, required for maintenance support of all equipment.
classesOfSupply.planningNotes: Drives field-maintenance throughput. Plan PLL (Prescribed Load List) at unit level and ASL at the SSA. Demand-driven with seasonal/operational variation.

classesOfSupply.title: Material to Support Nonmilitary Programs
classesOfSupply.description: Material to support nonmilitary programs such as agricultural and economic development, not included in Classes I through IX.
classesOfSupply.planningNotes: Common in stability and DSCA operations. Coordinate with G-9/CA, USAID, host-nation authorities, and supporting interagency partners.
